name: Cloud Instance Compromise Response
description: Responds to suspected cloud instance (EC2, GCE, Azure VM) compromise. Riggs AI analyzes the alert for indicators of compromise such as unusual outbound connections, credential harvesting from metadata service, or cryptomining behavior. Confirmed compromises trigger instance snapshot for forensic preservation, network isolation, forensic analysis of the snapshot, incident ticket creation, and team notification. Unconfirmed alerts receive documentation.
author: T1 Agentics

required_integrations.name: AWS Security Hub
required_integrations.reason: Source of instance compromise findings and API for snapshot/isolation operations

required_integrations.name: Jira
required_integrations.reason: Create forensic investigation tickets for confirmed instance compromises

required_integrations.name: Slack
required_integrations.reason: Notify the security team of instance compromise and forensic response actions

config.description: Triggers when suspicious behavior is detected on a cloud instance such as unusual outbound traffic, metadata service abuse, or known malware signatures

config.additional_instructions: Evaluate instance compromise indicators: metadata service access patterns, unusual outbound connections, known C2 IPs, cryptomining signatures, privilege escalation attempts
config.description: AI-powered analysis of cloud instance behavior to determine if the instance has been compromised
Routes based on whether the AI assessment confirmed the cloud instance is compromised
Create an EBS snapshot of all attached volumes for forensic preservation before any remediation actions
Network-isolate the compromised instance by replacing its security group with a deny-all group while preserving the instance for analysis

config.query: Analyze snapshot for instance={{trigger_1.alert.instance_id}}: running processes, open network connections, modified system files, installed packages delta, cron jobs, user accounts, SSH authorized_keys changes
config.description: Perform automated forensic analysis on the snapshot to identify malware, backdoors, and the full scope of compromise
Create a forensic investigation ticket with snapshot details and automated analysis results
Notify the security team of the instance compromise and all forensic response actions

parameters.note: Reviewed by Riggs AI (cloud_security template). Instance behavior determined non-malicious. Confidence: {{analyze_1.confidence}}. Assessment: {{analyze_1.summary}}

config.description: Document that AI analysis did not confirm an instance compromise
